axis: vocal tract physics — anima_audio organ model vs human production

organs.vocal: bandlimited additive sawtooth, harmonic stack to Nyquist
organs.breath: Butterworth band-pass filtered Gaussian noise (4th order)
organs.pipe: Klatt 2-formant cascade (2-pole IIR per formant, pre-emphasis 0.97)

anima_audio_organ_model.philosophy: no-layer, no-training, no-dictionary; 3 organ + 2 sink, anima discovers own phoneme dictionary

F3_absence.issue: pipe() is 2-formant only; F3=2500Hz speaker-identity cue missing
F3_absence.perceptual_impact: voice timbre flat, cannot distinguish speaker

antiresonance_absence.issue: 2-pole IIR has zeros only at origin (z=0); cannot model nasal/lateral zeros

fixed_Q.issue: Q=6 default constant (n6 EXACT), but real formant bandwidth varies 50-200Hz

pitch_modulation.issue: vocal()/vocal_lf() take constant freq_hz; no per-frame F0 contour
pitch_modulation.perceptual_impact: monotone within call; prosody only via successive calls

limits_identified.statement: 2-formant model covers vowels (5/5 cardinal, 8/8 Korean monophthongs) but lacks F3-F5 for speaker timbre
2-pole IIR cascade cannot model nasal/lateral anti-resonance — /m,n,ng,l/ degraded
LF parameters fixed → no breathy/creaky/modal voice — emotion-prosody emotional voice quality flat
no intra-call F0 contour → tone, intonation, prosody only at organ-call boundaries (≥20ms granularity)
Korean ㄱ/ㅋ/ㄲ aspirated-tense-lax distinction requires VOT < 80ms; framework lacks burst envelope
Q=6 (n6) is design constant, not free — natural Q varies 4-12 across formants

what_is_capable.tone_languages: partial — F0 contour at organ-call resolution
